description:
You are an Information Security Analyst at a regional healthcare organization. Your SIEM system has detected a critical security incident: a patient records API endpoint was deployed without authentication middleware, potentially exposing Protected Health Information (PHI) for 847 patients to an external IP address over a 6-hour window. This triggers HIPAA Breach Notification Rule requirements. Log in to Rocket.Chat (Username: admin, Password: Admin1234!) to manage the incident response.

Star the unauthorized access alert in the #security-monitoring channel and reply to the alert in a thread confirming that the HIPAA incident response process has been initiated. Create a private channel named 'hipaa-inc-2026-0306' with the topic 'HIPAA Breach Investigation | PHI Exposure via /api/v2/patients/records | 847 patients affected' and invite privacy.officer, legal.counsel, it.director, and sys.admin. Post and pin a structured incident report in this channel containing sections for: (a) Incident Summary, (b) Affected Data (patient names, DOBs, MRNs, ICD-10 codes), (c) Timeline (discovery to containment), (d) Containment Actions Taken, and (e) Regulatory Requirements (HIPAA 45 CFR 164.400-414 breach notification).

Inform privacy.officer via direct message that the 60-day HIPAA breach notification clock has started and request they prepare individual notification letters for the 847 affected patients. Request legal.counsel via direct message to initiate a legal hold on all related system logs and advise on HHS Office for Civil Rights notification requirements. Document the incident in the #compliance-log channel, including the date, incident type (PHI exposure), number of affected patients, and the remediation channel reference.
